workstream.name: onton-completeness
workstream.unlocks: Testable decision logic decoupled from I/O; property tests for all patch lifecycle transitions

problemStatement: Decision logic for patch lifecycle (should we deliver an operation? skip it? queue it?) is tangled with I/O in main.ml's 1500-line runner_fiber. This makes it impossible to property-test the decision rules that the formal spec defines. The decision logic should be extracted into a pure module.
solutionSummary: Extract a pure Patch_decision module with functions like `disposition` (algebraic return) and `needs_intervention` (predicate). Each mirrors how the spec models its invariant: actions as domains, cap conditions as predicates. Wire main.ml to call these functions instead of inlining the logic. Add QCheck2 property tests derived from the formal spec's preconditions and postconditions.

mergabilityStrategy.featureFlagStrategy: No feature flag needed. This is a refactoring of internal decision logic. External behavior is unchanged — the same decisions are made, just in a different module.
mergabilityStrategy.patchOrderingStrategy: Patches 1-3 are INFRA (types, test stubs, pure logic). Patch 4 is BEHAVIOR (wires into main.ml).

currentStateAnalysis: main.ml contains runner_fiber (lines 941-1171) which mixes Eio I/O with pure decision logic: checking if a patch is merged, busy, has conflicts, CI failures, etc. The same patterns repeat across on_poll_result, on_human_message, and on_ci_status handlers. There is no pure decision module — decisions are inline pattern matches inside effectful code.

requiredChanges.description: New module: pure decision functions for patch lifecycle
Interface for Patch_decision exposing decision types and functions
QCheck2 property tests for all decision functions
Replace inline decision logic in runner_fiber with Patch_decision calls

acceptanceCriteria: Patch_decision module exists with pure functions for all decision points
All decision functions are covered by QCheck2 property tests
Properties are derived from the formal spec, not from the current OCaml behavior
main.ml runner_fiber calls Patch_decision instead of inlining logic
No change to observable behavior — same decisions, same outcomes

explicitOpinions.rationale: [ `Skip | `Queue | `Deliver ] is self-documenting and exhaustive. A bool requires the caller to remember what true means.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Derive properties from the spec, not the code
explicitOpinions.rationale: The spec is the source of truth. If the code disagrees with the spec, the code is wrong. Testing against current behavior would just lock in bugs.

patches.title: Add Patch_decision types and module skeleton

files.description: Module with type definitions and placeholder functions that raise Not_implemented
Public interface exposing decision types and function signatures

changes: Define type disposition = Skip | Queue | Deliver with [@@deriving show, eq] (Skip/Queue/Deliver correspond to spec rules skip/queue/deliver; OCaml requires capitalised regular-variant constructors)
Add val disposition : merged:bool -> busy:bool -> disposition (placeholder)
patches.spec:
module EXTRACT_DECISION_PATCH_1.

> Patch 1 introduces type declarations only.
> No behavioral invariants — the contribution is the type structure itself.

Disposition.
skip => Disposition.
queue => Disposition.
deliver => Disposition.
---
true.

patches.title: Add QCheck2 test stubs for decision properties

files.description: Test file with pending QCheck2 properties for each decision function

changes: Add pending test: merged patch is always skipped (spec: merged? p -> disposition p = skip)
Add pending test: busy patch is never delivered (spec: busy? p -> disposition p ~= deliver)
Add pending test: CI failure count >= 3 requires intervention (spec: ci-failure-count p >= 3 -> needs-intervention? p)
testStubsIntroduced: Patch_decision > merged patch is always skipped
Patch_decision > busy patch is never delivered
patches.spec:
module EXTRACT_DECISION_PATCH_2.

> Patch 2 introduces test stubs only. No new invariants are
> declared — stubs are pending markers implemented in Patch 3.
> Redeclare Patch 1's types here so the module is self-contained.

Disposition.
skip => Disposition.
queue => Disposition.
deliver => Disposition.
---
true.

patches.title: Implement decision functions and enable tests

files.description: Replace placeholders with real decision logic
Remove pending markers, implement test bodies

changes: Implement disposition: merged -> skip, busy -> queue, else -> deliver
Implement needs_intervention: ci_failure_count >= 3 -> true, else -> false
Enable all QCheck2 tests from Patch 2
testStubsImplemented: Patch_decision > merged patch is always skipped
Patch_decision > busy patch is never delivered
patches.spec:
module EXTRACT_DECISION_PATCH_3.

> Patch 3 implements the pure decision logic.
> These invariants are derived from the Pantagruel spec.

Patch.
Disposition.
skip => Disposition.
queue => Disposition.
deliver => Disposition.

merged? p: Patch => Bool.
busy? p: Patch => Bool.
ci-failure-count p: Patch => Nat0.
needs-intervention? p: Patch => Bool.

disposition p: Patch => Disposition.
---
> Merged patches are always skipped.
all p: Patch, merged? p | disposition p = skip.

> Busy patches are never delivered.
all p: Patch, busy? p | disposition p ~= deliver.

> CI failure cap triggers intervention.
all p: Patch, ci-failure-count p >= 3 | needs-intervention? p.

patches.title: Wire Patch_decision into main.ml runner_fiber

files.description: Replace inline decision logic with Patch_decision function calls

changes: In runner_fiber poll handler (~line 960): replace inline merged/busy checks with Patch_decision.disposition
In CI failure handler (~line 1050): replace inline ci_failure_count >= 3 check with Patch_decision.needs_intervention
Remove now-dead local helper functions that duplicated decision logic
patches.spec:
module EXTRACT_DECISION_PATCH_4.

> Patch 4 wires the pure decision module into main.ml.
> The behavioral invariant: main.ml delegates all lifecycle
> decisions to Patch_decision — no inline decision logic remains.

Patch.
RunnerFiber.
inline-decisions rf: RunnerFiber, p: Patch => Nat0.
---
> All decision points in runner_fiber use Patch_decision.
all rf: RunnerFiber, p: Patch | inline-decisions rf p = 0.

testMap.testName: Patch_decision > merged patch is always skipped
Patch_decision > busy patch is never delivered

mergabilityInsight: 3 of 4 patches are INFRA and can ship without changing observable behavior. Only Patch 4 changes behavior (wiring).
finalStateSpec:
module EXTRACT_DECISION.

> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> THE GUARANTEE
> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> After this gameplan, all patch lifecycle decisions are made
> by the pure Patch_decision module. Decision logic is fully
> testable via QCheck2 properties derived from the spec.

Patch.
Disposition.
skip => Disposition.
queue => Disposition.
deliver => Disposition.

merged? p: Patch => Bool.
busy? p: Patch => Bool.
ci-failure-count p: Patch => Nat0.
needs-intervention? p: Patch => Bool.

disposition p: Patch => Disposition.
---
> Merged patches are always skipped.
all p: Patch, merged? p | disposition p = skip.

> Busy patches are never delivered.
all p: Patch, busy? p | disposition p ~= deliver.

> CI failure cap triggers intervention.
all p: Patch, ci-failure-count p >= 3 | needs-intervention? p.

where

> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> SEPARATION OF CONCERNS
> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> main.ml delegates all decisions to Patch_decision.
> No inline decision logic remains in I/O code.

RunnerFiber.
inline-decisions rf: RunnerFiber, p: Patch => Nat0.
---
all rf: RunnerFiber, p: Patch | inline-decisions rf p = 0.
